zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice de Craft於大約1202年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾維爾時，她的父親Roger de Craft34歲，母親Cecily de Turville32歲。她於1215年之前與William de Charnells在美國賓夕法尼亞州薩默塞特薩默塞特 堂史普赫斯本德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1240年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾艾爾莫斯索普享年40歲。

nameMeanings.name: Craft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever, learned’.
English: habitational name from Croft in Leicestershire. See also   Croft  .
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . See also   Croft  .

variants.name: Croft
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, for example in Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Herefordshire, and North Yorkshire, all named with Old English croft ‘small arable enclosure (normally adjoining a house)’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a croft, from Middle English croft.
English: possibly in some cases a variant of   Craft   2.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . Compare   Kroft   and   Craft  .

variants.name: Crafts
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of Craft , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Crafter
meanings: English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever’ in the sense ‘craftsman’. Compare Craft .

variants.name: Kraft
meanings: German (also Kräft), Danish, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a strong man, from Old High German kraft, German Kraft ‘strength, power’. The Swedish name probably originated as a soldier's name. In part, the German and Danish names possibly also derive from a late survival of the same word used as a byname, Old High German Chraft(o), Old Norse Kraptr. See also Craft and Croft .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice de Craft was born about 1202, in Willey, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger de Craft, was 34 and her mother, Cecily de Turville, was 32. She married William de Charnells before 1215, in Husband, Somerset Township, Somerset, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died about 1240, in Elmesthorpe, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 40.

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Craft(이)가 대략 1202년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 윌리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Craft(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Turville(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1215년 이전 미국 펜실베이니아 주 서머세트 서머세트 타운시프 후스반드에서 William de Charnells(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 엘메스소에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
